title: What's a Function? 🤖
description: Stop repeating code! Build reusable mini-machines.

slides.title: The Code Factory 🏭
slides.content:
# Reusable Machines

Imagine you have a complex block of code that calculates a player's score. But you need to calculate scores in 5 different places in your app!

Instead of copy-pasting the same 10 lines of code everywhere (which is a nightmare to update), we can bundle it into a **Function**.

A function is simply a named block of code that you can run whenever and wherever you want.

slides.title: You've already used one! 😱
slides.content:
## `main()`

Remember the `void main() { ... }` block that starts every Dart program?

That is a function! It is simply a special function that Dart automatically knows to look for when the app launches.

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of putting code inside a function?

options.text: It completely hides the code from users.
It lets you reuse the code anywhere just by calling its name, rather than typing it out again.
It makes the code run instantly on launch.

quizData.explanation: Functions prevent you from repeating yourself (DRY principle). You write it once, and reuse it forever.

slides.title: Writing a custom function ✍️

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to ACTUALLY RUN (or 'call') this function inside main():
___();
quizData.explanation: To call a function, simply state its name followed by parentheses: sayHello();

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Look how easy it is to reuse the code 3 times!
